name: European Championships
description: UEFA Euros and international European football

questions.q: Which color is most traditionally associated with the Dutch national football team?

source.notes: A reference to the Dutch royal House of Orange-Nassau.

questions.q: In football, what does the 'U' in UEFA stand for?

source.notes: UEFA = Union of European Football Associations.

questions.q: The Dutch national team's ardent fans are nicknamed what?

source.notes: Sometimes also called 'Oranje' after the color.

questions.q: Which European country's football shirt is famous for its red-and-white chequered pattern?

source.notes: The 'šahovnica' (chessboard) pattern appears on the Croatian coat of arms.

questions.q: Gareth Bale was the star forward for which national football team?

source.notes: Retired 2023; Wales's all-time top scorer.

questions.q: What is the name of Scotland's historic national football stadium in Glasgow?

source.notes: Located in the Mount Florida district of Glasgow.

questions.q: England famously won the FIFA World Cup in which year, the only time they have won it?

source.notes: Beat West Germany 4-2 at Wembley.

questions.q: UEFA, the governing body of European football, is headquartered in which country?

source.notes: Based in Nyon, Switzerland since 1995.

questions.q: What is the name of the award given to the top goalkeeper at a FIFA World Cup?

source.notes: Formally the 'Yashin Trophy' until 2010, then renamed the Golden Glove.

questions.q: Peter Shilton is England's most capped player, with how many international appearances?

source.notes: Goalkeeper; played 125 internationals between 1970 and 1990.

questions.q: In 2019, which Portuguese forward became the first European player to score 100 international goals?

source.notes: Now holds the all-time international goals record for any male player.

questions.q: Portugal won the inaugural edition of the UEFA Nations League held in which year?

source.notes: Won 1-0 against the Netherlands in Porto, June 2019.

questions.q: Legendary goalkeeper Peter Schmeichel played international football for which country?

source.notes: Denmark won their only Euros in 1992 with Schmeichel in goal.

questions.q: Which country hosted the FIFA World Cup in 2018?

source.notes: France won by beating Croatia 4-2 in the final.

questions.q: Which European country hosted the FIFA World Cup in 1982?

source.notes: Italy won by beating West Germany 3-1 in the final at the Santiago Bernabéu.

questions.q: Zinedine Zidane, midfield maestro of the 1998 World Cup winners, played for which national side?

source.notes: Scored twice in the 1998 World Cup Final against Brazil.

questions.q: Which bird of prey features on the Polish national football team's badge?

source.notes: The White Eagle is Poland's national symbol.

questions.q: Which country shocked Europe by winning Euro 2004, pulling off a huge upset as hosts Portugal were beaten in the final?

source.notes: Coached by Otto Rehhagel; beat Portugal 1-0 in the final.

questions.q: Wales recorded one of football's biggest ranking jumps, rising from around 117th in 2011 to which top-10 position by 2015?

source.notes: Peaked at No. 8 in October 2015.

questions.q: Who is the Republic of Ireland's most capped player, with 146 appearances?

source.notes: Also Ireland's all-time top scorer with 68 goals.

questions.q: The world's first-ever international football match was played in 1872 between England and which other country?

source.notes: Played November 30, 1872 at Hamilton Crescent in Partick, Glasgow. Ended 0-0.

questions.q: Which Central Asian country joined UEFA in 2002 despite being roughly 90% located in Asia?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: stem narrowed to Central Asia + 2002 UEFA admission to uniquely identify Kazakhstan (Turkey/Armenia/Azerbaijan are also largely in Asia).
